articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

article.description: Bayern Munich extended their perfect Bundesliga start when Michael Olise scored two goals and set up two more against Werder Bremen.

images.name: Bayern stay hot in 5-0 thumping of Werder Bremen
images.caption: A ruthless Bayern Munich continues their hot form in a comfortable 5-0 win over Werder Bremen in the Bundesliga.

video.headline: Bayern stay hot in 5-0 thumping of Werder Bremen
video.caption: A ruthless Bayern Munich continues their hot form in a comfortable 5-0 win over Werder Bremen in the Bundesliga.
video.title: Bayern stay hot in 5-0 thumping of Werder Bremen
video.description: A ruthless Bayern Munich continues their hot form in a comfortable 5-0 win over Werder Bremen in the Bundesliga.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Bayern stay hot in 5-0 thumping of Werder Bremen) 2024/09/21 ESHEET

article.story:
Bayern Munich extended their perfect Bundesliga start when Michael Olise scored two goals and set up two more to give them a 5-0 victory over Werder Bremen in a one-sided contest at the Weserstadion on Saturday.

Bayern, who suffered a shock 1-0 defeat to Bremen when the sides last met in a league clash in January, ran out comfortable winners to move up to 12 points from four matches.

Winger Olise, signed from Premier League club Crystal Palace for a reported fee of €60 million ($67m) in July, has quickly established himself in the first team. He once again repaid manager Vincent Kompany's faith as he was heavily involved in Bayern's attack.

Bayern, who have scored 20 goals in three matches across all competitions, opened the scoring in the 23rd minute when Harry Kane seized possession on the edge of the area and played the ball to Olise, who fired his shot past keeper Michael Zetterer.

The France winger then wriggled his way into Bremen's penalty area in the 32nd minute and his cutback was turned home by Jamal Musiala.

Bayern continued their onslaught after the break and Olise teed up Kane for a shot from the edge of the box, which led to the striker's 41st Bundesliga goal -- making him the highest-scoring English player in the German top flight's history.

Olise netted his fifth goal in three matches with a delightful side-footed finish into the top corner and substitute Serge Gnabry wrapped up the scoring in the 65th minute.

Bayern were without veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer, who was named in the starting lineup before pulling out after the warm-up due to a thigh injury, but the change made little difference.

Deputy Sven Ulreich was untroubled throughout the match and faced no shots on target from a toothless Bremen attack.

Bayern stepped off the gas in the last 15 minutes of the match, likely saving energy for a testing clash against champions Bayer Leverkusen on Sept. 28.
